_metadata.title: ComplianceLint Interim Standard for EU AI Act Article 12: Record-keeping
_metadata.disclaimer: This is an interim standard developed by ComplianceLint based on available guidance, industry best practices, and the draft ISO/IEC DIS 24970:2025. It is NOT an official standard. It will be superseded when CEN-CENELEC publishes the harmonized standard for Article 12 (expected Q4 2026).
basis: ISO 27001 Annex A.12.4 — Logging and monitoring
_metadata.awaiting_standard: CEN-CENELEC harmonized standard for Art. 12 (expected Q4 2026)

requirements.title: Logging Framework Required
requirements.requirement: The AI system MUST use a structured logging framework capable of automatic event recording.
requirements.rationale: Art. 12(1) requires 'automatic recording of events' — ad-hoc print statements or manual logging do not satisfy this requirement.
acceptable_implementations: Python: structlog, loguru, stdlib logging with JSON formatter
not_acceptable: Manual log file writing without framework

requirements.title: Logging Coverage
requirements.requirement: At least 90% of API endpoints / entry points MUST have structured logging.
requirements.rationale: Art. 12(1) says 'over the duration of the lifetime' — gaps in logging coverage mean some events are not recorded.
requirements.note: 90% threshold is ComplianceLint interim standard. Official threshold TBD by CEN-CENELEC.

requirements.title: Structured Log Fields
requirements.requirement: Each log entry MUST include at minimum: timestamp, event_type, and a correlation identifier (session_id, trace_id, or request_id).
recommended_fields: input_hash (hash of input data, not raw data)
requirements.rationale: Art. 12(2)(a) requires identifying risk situations — this requires structured fields that enable querying and anomaly detection, not just text messages.

requirements.title: Log Retention
requirements.requirement: Logs MUST be retained for a minimum of 6 months. Systems in healthcare, law enforcement, or critical infrastructure SHOULD retain for 12+ months.
requirements.rationale: Art. 19(1) explicitly states 'at least six months.' Longer retention for high-impact domains is industry best practice.
implementation_guidance: Configure log rotation with minimum 180-day retention
Use append-only log storage (S3 with object lock, WORM storage)
Ensure retention policy is documented and auditable
Consider GDPR data minimization: anonymize personal data in logs after active use period

requirements.title: Log Integrity / Tamper Protection
requirements.requirement: Logs SHOULD be protected against tampering. For high-risk biometric systems (Annex III, 1(a)), logs MUST be tamper-protected.
requirements.rationale: Multiple expert sources interpret Art. 12's 'automatic recording' requirement as implying forensic-grade logs. If logs can be modified after the fact, they cannot serve their purpose of enabling traceability (Recital 71).
acceptable_implementations: Hash chain (each log entry includes hash of previous entry)
Append-only storage (S3 Object Lock, Azure Immutable Blob)
requirements.note: This is elevated from SHOULD to MUST for biometric systems based on the heightened requirements in Art. 12(4).

requirements.title: Log Accessibility for Deployers
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST offer deployers a mechanism to access operational logs or log summaries relevant to monitoring.
requirements.rationale: Art. 12(2)(c) requires logging that enables deployer monitoring per Art. 26(5). If deployers cannot access logs, this obligation cannot be fulfilled.
acceptable_implementations: Log forwarding to deployer's SIEM/logging system

scoring.partial: R01 met + at least 3 of R02-R06 met
scoring.non_compliant: R01 not met, or fewer than 3 of R02-R06 met
